rationale: The signals show strong, consistent demand around three core areas: (1) morph identification and genetics across multiple specific morphs (cappuccino, lilly white, axanthic), (2) breeding mechanics and outcomes, and (3) emerging trends like albino interest and diet awareness. Google Trends show +300% rise in "repashy crested gecko diet" and +130% for "albino," while YouTube has significant viewership on morph guides and cappuccino breeding controversies. Bluesky engagement clusters around morph aesthetics and breeding discussions. However, most general care, broad morph lists, and established breeding topics are either already published or too generic. I'm focusing on three concrete, actionable angles that address actual breeder confusion: (1) why albino demand is rising but remains genetically unclear/risky, (2) frappuccino as an under-covered outcome of cappuccino pairings, and (3) identifying lilly white juveniles before markers fully express. All three target breeding decisions and avoid duplicating existing slugs.
